description: AksharaMD downstream LLM accuracy benchmark corpus — v0.3.3

judges.note: AksharaMD vs MarkItDown, 12 documents per format
AksharaMD vs MarkItDown, ~8 documents per format

pdf.selection: Spanning simple single-column, multi-column layout, table-heavy, and math-heavy papers

docx.selection: Spanning short single-section through multi-chapter documents with embedded tables

pptx.selection: Spanning minimal-text through data-heavy slide decks

xlsx.selection: Spanning single-sheet through multi-sheet workbooks with varying cell density

html.selection: Long-form encyclopedia articles with heavy navigation markup to stress boilerplate removal

epub.selection: Public domain books spanning fiction, non-fiction, and technical works

txt.selection: Plain-text versions of public domain books; same titles as EPUB set where available

eml.selection: Spanning plain-text, HTML-body, and multi-part messages with attachments

ipynb.selection: Notebooks with varying ratios of code cells to markdown cells

csv.selection: Files with row counts from dozens to thousands; varying column complexity

json.selection: Flat through deeply nested structures; varying key and value verbosity

xml.selection: Small, medium, and large files; varying attribute and element density

qa_generation.question_type: Factual — specific, verifiable facts: names, numbers, dates, identifiers

evaluation_protocol.context: First 6000 characters of each tool's output (~4000 tokens)
evaluation_protocol.scoring: 0–10 per answer; 10 = fully correct, 0 = wrong or irrelevant

reproduce.token_only: python -m benchmarks.llm_qa_eval --qa benchmarks/eval_corpus_qa.yaml --tools aksharamd markitdown llamaparse pymupdf4llm docling --no-llm
reproduce.full: python -m benchmarks.llm_qa_eval --qa benchmarks/eval_corpus_qa.yaml --tools aksharamd markitdown llamaparse pymupdf4llm docling --llms claude
